ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opp_clean AS (
    SELECT
        o.opp_kennung,
        INITCAP(TRIM(o.titel)) AS titel,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) AS vertriebsphase,
        o.zieldatum,
        o.auftragswert,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) AS waehrungscode_raw,
        TRIM(o.kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref_raw
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
),
parsed_date AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum,
        auftragswert,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS zieldatum_parsed,
        CASE
            WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
            ELSE auftragswert
        END AS auftragswert_clean
    FROM opp_clean
),
amount_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum_parsed,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, '.', 1)) > LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert_clean, ',', '', 'g'), ',', 1)) + LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, ',', 2)) THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), '\.', '', 'g'), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND auftragswert_clean !~ '\.' THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        END AS amount_val
    FROM parsed_date
)
SELECT
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(opp_kennung)) AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROSPECTING', 'PROSPEKTING') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('QUALIFICATION', 'QUALIFIZIERUNG') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEEDS ANALYSIS', 'BEDARFSANALYSE') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('VALUE PROPOSITION', 'WERTVORSCHLAG', 'VALUE_PROPOSITION') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('ID DECISION MAKERS', 'ENTSCHEIDER IDENTIFIZIEREN', 'ID_DECISION_MAKERS') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PERCEPTION ANALYSIS', 'WAHRNEHMUNGSANALYSE') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE', 'ANGEBOT', 'PROPOSAL_PRICE_QUOTE', 'PROPOSAL / PRICE QUOTE') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEGOTIATION/REVIEW', 'VERHANDLUNG', 'NEGOTIATION_REVIEW', 'NEGOTIATION / REVIEW') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED WON', 'ABGESCHLOSSEN GEWINNT', 'CLOSED_WON', 'WON') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED LOST', 'VERLOREN', 'CLOSED_LOST', 'LOST') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    zieldatum_parsed AS "CloseDate",
    amount_val AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('USD', 'US-DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('GBP', 'POUND') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('CHF', 'SWISS FRANC') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(kunden_ref_raw)) AS TEXT) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2024-01-01' AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM amount_parsed
', tool_call_id='call_a4l9ziei', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 22, 219328, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 22, 219737, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13db13c8e577', conversation_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13da8c28a5ca')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The error says "invalid input syntax for type double precision: 'None'". This means somewhere in the source data, `auftragswert` contains the literal string "None" which is not being handled.

Looking at the `parsed_date` CTE:
```sql
WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
```

If the value is "None" (no space after), it won't match this pattern. Then in `amount_parsed`:
```sql
ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
```

"None" would fail to cast to double precision.

I need to add a check for "None" strings (case-insensitive) and treat them as NULL, or strip them out properly.

Let me also check if there are other source tables that might have similar issues. Let me first look at what the opportunity model looks like more carefully.

The fix: in `amount_parsed`, I should add a CASE to check if `auftragswert_clean` matches 'None' (case insensitive) before trying to cast. Or better, handle "None" in the `parsed_date` CTE by treating it as NULL/empty.

Let me fix this by checking for "None"-like values in the amount parsing logic. I'll add a condition that checks if the value, after cleaning, is still not numeric-like and set it to NULL.

Actually, looking more carefully:
1. If `auftragswert` = "None", it doesn't match `'^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s'` (no trailing space), so it goes to ELSE branch and stays as "None"
2. Then in amount_parsed, "None" doesn't contain dots or commas, so it hits the ELSE: `TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION` which fails

The fix should be to check for "None"-like strings (case-insensitive) before casting. I'll add a check like:
```sql
WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert_clean)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '') THEN NULL
```

Or more robustly, check that the string is numeric-like before casting:
```sql
CASE
    WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert_clean)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL') THEN NULL
    WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' ... 
    ...
```

Let me write the fixed model.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/opportunity.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_vo1915no')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=2939, output_tokens=608), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 43, 797591, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 43, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-af87544a-8854-436f-a142-6bbf3ab8ca6a', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13db13c8e577', conversation_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13da8c28a5ca')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 9, 702141, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- opportunity: Database Error in model opportunity (models/opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: "None"
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 9, 702149, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 9, 702461, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13db13c8e577', conversation_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13da8c28a5ca')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opp_clean AS (
    SELECT
        o.opp_kennung,
        INITCAP(TRIM(o.titel)) AS titel,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) AS vertriebsphase,
        o.zieldatum,
        o.auftragswert,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) AS waehrungscode_raw,
        TRIM(o.kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref_raw
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
),
parsed_date AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum,
        auftragswert,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS zieldatum_parsed,
        CASE
            WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
            ELSE auftragswert
        END AS auftragswert_clean
    FROM opp_clean
),
amount_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum_parsed,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, '.', 1)) > LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert_clean, ',', '', 'g'), ',', 1)) + LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, ',', 2)) THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), '\.', '', 'g'), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND auftragswert_clean !~ '\.' THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        END AS amount_val
    FROM parsed_date
)
SELECT
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(opp_kennung)) AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROSPECTING', 'PROSPEKTING') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('QUALIFICATION', 'QUALIFIZIERUNG') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEEDS ANALYSIS', 'BEDARFSANALYSE') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('VALUE PROPOSITION', 'WERTVORSCHLAG', 'VALUE_PROPOSITION') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('ID DECISION MAKERS', 'ENTSCHEIDER IDENTIFIZIEREN', 'ID_DECISION_MAKERS') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PERCEPTION ANALYSIS', 'WAHRNEHMUNGSANALYSE') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE', 'ANGEBOT', 'PROPOSAL_PRICE_QUOTE', 'PROPOSAL / PRICE QUOTE') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEGOTIATION/REVIEW', 'VERHANDLUNG', 'NEGOTIATION_REVIEW', 'NEGOTIATION / REVIEW') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED WON', 'ABGESCHLOSSEN GEWINNT', 'CLOSED_WON', 'WON') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED LOST', 'VERLOREN', 'CLOSED_LOST', 'LOST') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    zieldatum_parsed AS "CloseDate",
    amount_val AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('USD', 'US-DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('GBP', 'POUND') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('CHF', 'SWISS FRANC') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(kunden_ref_raw)) AS TEXT) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2024-01-01' AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM amount_parsed
', tool_call_id='call_a4l9ziei', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 22, 219328, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 43, 22, 219737, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13db13c8e577', conversation_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13da8c28a5ca')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opp_clean AS (
    SELECT
        o.opp_kennung,
        INITCAP(TRIM(o.titel)) AS titel,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) AS vertriebsphase,
        o.zieldatum,
        o.auftragswert,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) AS waehrungscode_raw,
        TRIM(o.kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref_raw
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
),
parsed_date AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum,
        auftragswert,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS zieldatum_parsed,
        CASE
            WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
            ELSE auftragswert
        END AS auftragswert_clean
    FROM opp_clean
),
amount_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum_parsed,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert_clean)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, '.', 1)) > LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert_clean, ',', '', 'g'), ',', 1)) + LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, ',', 2)) THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), '\.', '', 'g'), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND auftragswert_clean !~ '\.' THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN NOT (auftragswert_clean ~ '^[0-9.,\-]+$') THEN NULL
            ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        END AS amount_val
    FROM parsed_date
)
SELECT
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(opp_kennung)) AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROSPECTING', 'PROSPEKTING') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('QUALIFICATION', 'QUALIFIZIERUNG') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEEDS ANALYSIS', 'BEDARFSANALYSE') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('VALUE PROPOSITION', 'WERTVORSCHLAG', 'VALUE_PROPOSITION') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('ID DECISION MAKERS', 'ENTSCHEIDER IDENTIFIZIEREN', 'ID_DECISION_MAKERS') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PERCEPTION ANALYSIS', 'WAHRNEHMUNGSANALYSE') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE', 'ANGEBOT', 'PROPOSAL_PRICE_QUOTE', 'PROPOSAL / PRICE QUOTE') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEGOTIATION/REVIEW', 'VERHANDLUNG', 'NEGOTIATION_REVIEW', 'NEGOTIATION / REVIEW') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED WON', 'ABGESCHLOSSEN GEWINNT', 'CLOSED_WON', 'WON') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED LOST', 'VERLOREN', 'CLOSED_LOST', 'LOST') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    zieldatum_parsed AS "CloseDate",
    amount_val AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('USD', 'US-DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('GBP', 'POUND') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('CHF', 'SWISS FRANC') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(kunden_ref_raw)) AS TEXT) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2024-01-01' AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM amount_parsed', tool_call_id='call_wo5am6jl', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 45, 38, 862113, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 45, 38, 862533, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13db13c8e577', conversation_id='019f3ef6-cd84-762f-9992-13da8c28a5ca')

- REGEXP_REPLACE("400.902,63", ',', '') = "400.90263", SPLIT_PART on ',' = "400.90263" → length 9
- SPLIT_PART("400.902,63", ',', 2) = "63" → length 2
- So: 3 > 9 + 2 = 3 > 11 → FALSE

2. `UPPER(TRIM(...)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-')` → NO ("400.902,63")
3. First complex WHEN with dots and commas → FALSE (as calculated above)
4. `auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND auftragswert_clean !~ '\.'` → Has both `,` and `.`, so FALSE
5. `NOT (auftragswert_clean ~ '^[0-9.,\-]+$')` → The string only has digits, dots, and commas, so this is FALSE
6. ELSE: `TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION` → tries to cast "400.902,63" which fails!

The fix should be simpler. When both `.` and `,` are present in a number string, the last separator is always the decimal point. So:
- Remove all dots (thousand separators)
- Replace comma with dot (decimal separator)

This gives us "400902.63" which is correct.

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 18, 213355, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- opportunity: Database Error in model opportunity (models/opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: "400.902,63"
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 18, 213365, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 18, 213723, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d9653cd0c187', conversation_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d964eb1c17ef')

WITH opp_clean AS (
    SELECT
        o.opp_kennung,
        INITCAP(TRIM(o.titel)) AS titel,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) AS vertriebsphase,
        o.zieldatum,
        o.auftragswert,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) AS waehrungscode_raw,
        TRIM(o.kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref_raw
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
),
parsed_date AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum,
        auftragswert,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS zieldatum_parsed,
        CASE
            WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
            ELSE auftragswert
        END AS auftragswert_clean
    FROM opp_clean
),
amount_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum_parsed,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert_clean)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, '.', 1)) > LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert_clean, ',', '', 'g'), ',', 1)) + LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, ',', 2)) THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), '\.', '', 'g'), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND auftragswert_clean !~ '\.' THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN NOT (auftragswert_clean ~ '^[0-9.,\-]+$') THEN NULL
            ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        END AS amount_val
    FROM parsed_date
)
SELECT
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(opp_kennung)) AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROSPECTING', 'PROSPEKTING') THEN 'Prospecting'

        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEEDS ANALYSIS', 'BEDARFSANALYSE') THEN 'Needs Analysis'

        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('ID DECISION MAKERS', 'ENTSCHEIDER IDENTIFIZIEREN', 'ID_DECISION_MAKERS') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PERCEPTION ANALYSIS', 'WAHRNEHMUNGSANALYSE') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE', 'ANGEBOT', 'PROPOSAL_PRICE_QUOTE', 'PROPOSAL / PRICE QUOTE') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEGOTIATION/REVIEW', 'VERHANDLUNG', 'NEGOTIATION_REVIEW', 'NEGOTIATION / REVIEW') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED WON', 'ABGESCHLOSSEN GEWINNT', 'CLOSED_WON', 'WON') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED LOST', 'VERLOREN', 'CLOSED_LOST', 'LOST') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    zieldatum_parsed AS "CloseDate",
    amount_val AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('USD', 'US-DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('GBP', 'POUND') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('CHF', 'SWISS FRANC') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(kunden_ref_raw)) AS TEXT) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2024-01-01' AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM amount_parsed', tool_call_id='call_2v7tqua5', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 30, 860832, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 30, 861247, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d9653cd0c187', conversation_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d964eb1c17ef')

So the condition fails and it falls through to other branches. The issue is my logic for detecting European format is flawed.

A simpler approach for detecting European number format: if a string has both `.` and `,`, and the `.` comes before the `,` in the string, then `.` is likely a thousand separator and `,` is the decimal separator.

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 18, 213355, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opp_clean AS (
    SELECT
        o.opp_kennung,
        INITCAP(TRIM(o.titel)) AS titel,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) AS vertriebsphase,
        o.zieldatum,
        o.auftragswert,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) AS waehrungscode_raw,
        TRIM(o.kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref_raw
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
),
parsed_date AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum,
        auftragswert,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS zieldatum_parsed,
        CASE
            WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
            ELSE auftragswert
        END AS auftragswert_clean
    FROM opp_clean
),
amount_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum_parsed,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert_clean)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, '.', 1)) > LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert_clean, ',', '', 'g'), ',', 1)) + LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, ',', 2)) THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), '\.', '', 'g'), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND auftragswert_clean !~ '\.' THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN NOT (auftragswert_clean ~ '^[0-9.,\-]+$') THEN NULL
            ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        END AS amount_val
    FROM parsed_date
)
SELECT
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(opp_kennung)) AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROSPECTING', 'PROSPEKTING') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('QUALIFICATION', 'QUALIFIZIERUNG') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEEDS ANALYSIS', 'BEDARFSANALYSE') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('VALUE PROPOSITION', 'WERTVORSCHLAG', 'VALUE_PROPOSITION') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('ID DECISION MAKERS', 'ENTSCHEIDER IDENTIFIZIEREN', 'ID_DECISION_MAKERS') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PERCEPTION ANALYSIS', 'WAHRNEHMUNGSANALYSE') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE', 'ANGEBOT', 'PROPOSAL_PRICE_QUOTE', 'PROPOSAL / PRICE QUOTE') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEGOTIATION/REVIEW', 'VERHANDLUNG', 'NEGOTIATION_REVIEW', 'NEGOTIATION / REVIEW') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED WON', 'ABGESCHLOSSEN GEWINNT', 'CLOSED_WON', 'WON') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED LOST', 'VERLOREN', 'CLOSED_LOST', 'LOST') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    zieldatum_parsed AS "CloseDate",
    amount_val AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('USD', 'US-DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('GBP', 'POUND') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('CHF', 'SWISS FRANC') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(kunden_ref_raw)) AS TEXT) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2024-01-01' AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM amount_parsed', tool_call_id='call_2v7tqua5', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 30, 860832, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 30, 861247, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d9653cd0c187', conversation_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d964eb1c17ef')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 18, 213355, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:

  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 18, 213365, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 18, 213723, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d9653cd0c187', conversation_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d964eb1c17ef')

        INITCAP(TRIM(o.titel)) AS titel,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) AS vertriebsphase,
        o.zieldatum,
        o.auftragswert,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) AS waehrungscode_raw,
        TRIM(o.kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref_raw
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
),
parsed_date AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum,
        auftragswert,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS zieldatum_parsed,
        CASE
            WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
            ELSE auftragswert
        END AS auftragswert_clean
    FROM opp_clean
),
amount_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum_parsed,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert_clean)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, '.', 1)) > LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert_clean, ',', '', 'g'), ',', 1)) + LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, ',', 2)) THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), '\.', '', 'g'), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND auftragswert_clean !~ '\.' THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN NOT (auftragswert_clean ~ '^[0-9.,\-]+$') THEN NULL
            ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        END AS amount_val
    FROM parsed_date
)
SELECT
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(opp_kennung)) AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROSPECTING', 'PROSPEKTING') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('QUALIFICATION', 'QUALIFIZIERUNG') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEEDS ANALYSIS', 'BEDARFSANALYSE') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('VALUE PROPOSITION', 'WERTVORSCHLAG', 'VALUE_PROPOSITION') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('ID DECISION MAKERS', 'ENTSCHEIDER IDENTIFIZIEREN', 'ID_DECISION_MAKERS') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PERCEPTION ANALYSIS', 'WAHRNEHMUNGSANALYSE') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE', 'ANGEBOT', 'PROPOSAL_PRICE_QUOTE', 'PROPOSAL / PRICE QUOTE') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEGOTIATION/REVIEW', 'VERHANDLUNG', 'NEGOTIATION_REVIEW', 'NEGOTIATION / REVIEW') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED WON', 'ABGESCHLOSSEN GEWINNT', 'CLOSED_WON', 'WON') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED LOST', 'VERLOREN', 'CLOSED_LOST', 'LOST') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    zieldatum_parsed AS "CloseDate",
    amount_val AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('USD', 'US-DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('GBP', 'POUND') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('CHF', 'SWISS FRANC') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(kunden_ref_raw)) AS TEXT) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2024-01-01' AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM amount_parsed', tool_call_id='call_2v7tqua5', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 30, 860832, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 30, 861247, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d9653cd0c187', conversation_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d964eb1c17ef')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 18, 213355, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- opportunity: Database Error in model opportunity (models/opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: "400.902,63"

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opp_clean AS (
    SELECT
        o.opp_kennung,
        INITCAP(TRIM(o.titel)) AS titel,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) AS vertriebsphase,
        o.zieldatum,
        o.auftragswert,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) AS waehrungscode_raw,
        TRIM(o.kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref_raw
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
),
parsed_date AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum,
        auftragswert,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS zieldatum_parsed,
        CASE
            WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
            ELSE auftragswert
        END AS auftragswert_clean
    FROM opp_clean
),
amount_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum_parsed,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert_clean)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, '.', 1)) > LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert_clean, ',', '', 'g'), ',', 1)) + LENGTH(SPLIT_PART(auftragswert_clean, ',', 2)) THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), '\.', '', 'g'), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND auftragswert_clean !~ '\.' THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            WHEN NOT (auftragswert_clean ~ '^[0-9.,\-]+$') THEN NULL
            ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        END AS amount_val
    FROM parsed_date
)
SELECT
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(opp_kennung)) AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROSPECTING', 'PROSPEKTING') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('QUALIFICATION', 'QUALIFIZIERUNG') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEEDS ANALYSIS', 'BEDARFSANALYSE') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('VALUE PROPOSITION', 'WERTVORSCHLAG', 'VALUE_PROPOSITION') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('ID DECISION MAKERS', 'ENTSCHEIDER IDENTIFIZIEREN', 'ID_DECISION_MAKERS') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PERCEPTION ANALYSIS', 'WAHRNEHMUNGSANALYSE') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE', 'ANGEBOT', 'PROPOSAL_PRICE_QUOTE', 'PROPOSAL / PRICE QUOTE') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEGOTIATION/REVIEW', 'VERHANDLUNG', 'NEGOTIATION_REVIEW', 'NEGOTIATION / REVIEW') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED WON', 'ABGESCHLOSSEN GEWINNT', 'CLOSED_WON', 'WON') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED LOST', 'VERLOREN', 'CLOSED_LOST', 'LOST') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    zieldatum_parsed AS "CloseDate",
    amount_val AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('USD', 'US-DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('GBP', 'POUND') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('CHF', 'SWISS FRANC') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(kunden_ref_raw)) AS TEXT) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2024-01-01' AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM amount_parsed', tool_call_id='call_2v7tqua5', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 30, 860832, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 46, 30, 861247, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d9653cd0c187', conversation_id='019f3ef9-ade4-725e-ae14-d964eb1c17ef')

A simpler approach for detecting European number format: if a string has both `.` and `,`, and the `.` comes before the `,` in the string, then `.` is likely a thousand separator and `,` is the decimal separator.

END AS amount_val

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).

- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.

- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.

Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).

- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opp_clean AS (
    SELECT
        o.opp_kennung,
        INITCAP(TRIM(o.titel)) AS titel,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) AS vertriebsphase,
        o.zieldatum,
        o.auftragswert,
        UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) AS waehrungscode_raw,
        TRIM(o.kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref_raw
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
),
parsed_date AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum,
        auftragswert,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum
            WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS zieldatum_parsed,
        CASE
            WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            WHEN auftragswert ~ '^\s*[A-Za-z]+\s' THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '[^0-9.,\-]', '', 'g')
            ELSE auftragswert
        END AS auftragswert_clean
    FROM opp_clean
),
amount_parsed AS (
    SELECT
        opp_kennung,
        titel,
        vertriebsphase,
        zieldatum_parsed,
        waehrungscode_raw,
        kunden_ref_raw,
        CASE
            WHEN TRIM(auftragswert_clean) = '' OR auftragswert_clean IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN UPPER(TRIM(auftragswert_clean)) IN ('NONE', 'N/A', 'NULL', '-') THEN NULL
            -- European format: dot before comma (e.g., "400.902,63")
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ '\.' AND auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND POSITION('.' IN auftragswert_clean) < POSITION(',' IN auftragswert_clean) THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), '\.', '', 'g'), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            -- Comma-only decimal (e.g., "1234,56")
            WHEN auftragswert_clean ~ ',' AND NOT (auftragswert_clean ~ '\.') THEN
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(auftragswert_clean), ',', '.', 'g')::DOUBLE PRECISION
            -- Pure numeric or US format (e.g., "1234.56")
            ELSE TRIM(auftragswert_clean)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        END AS amount_val
    FROM parsed_date
)
SELECT
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(opp_kennung)) AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROSPECTING', 'PROSPEKTING') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('QUALIFICATION', 'QUALIFIZIERUNG') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEEDS ANALYSIS', 'BEDARFSANALYSE') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN verbiebsphase IN ('VALUE PROPOSITION', 'WERTVORSCHLAG', 'VALUE_PROPOSITION') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('ID DECISION MAKERS', 'ENTSCHEIDER IDENTIFIZIEREN', 'ID_DECISION_MAKERS') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PERCEPTION ANALYSIS', 'WAHRNEHMUNGSANALYSE') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE', 'ANGEBOT', 'PROPOSAL_PRICE_QUOTE', 'PROPOSAL / PRICE QUOTE') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('NEGOTIATION/REVIEW', 'VERHANDLUNG', 'NEGOTIATION_REVIEW', 'NEGOTIATION / REVIEW') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED WON', 'ABGESCHLOSSEN GEWINNT', 'CLOSED_WON', 'WON') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN vertriebsphase IN ('CLOSED LOST', 'VERLOREN', 'CLOSED_LOST', 'LOST') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    zieldatum_parsed AS "CloseDate",
    amount_val AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw = 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('USD', 'US-DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('GBP', 'POUND') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN waehrungscode_raw IN ('CHF', 'SWISS FRANC') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CAST(UPPER(TRIM(kunden_ref_raw)) AS TEXT) AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(opp_kennung) AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2024-01-01' AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM amount_parsed
', tool_call_id='call_ww762mrg', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 48, 42, 729182, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 48, 42, 729612, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3efb-aed8-708f-8227-5495410f3f1d', conversation_id='019f3efb-aed8-708f-8227-54942b53a996')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 48, 29, 529753, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- opportunity: Database Error in model opportunity (models/opportunity.sql)
  column "verbiebsphase" does not exist
  LINE 78:         WHEN verbiebsphase IN ('VALUE PROPOSITION', 'WERTVOR...
                        ^
  HINT:  Perhaps you meant to reference the column "amount_parsed.vertriebsphase".
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 48, 29, 529761, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 7, 23, 48, 29, 530056, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f3efb-aed8-708f-8227-5495410f3f1d', conversation_id='019f3efb-aed8-708f-8227-54942b53a996')
